title: Sex Pies And Idiot Scrapes

jokes.text: I love how they made the river green.
jokes.setup: Character admires what appears to be festive St. Patrick's Day decoration
jokes.punchline: Next line reveals it's actually pollution from the nuclear plant
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons environmental satire setup - innocent observation leading to dark reality

jokes.text: Actually, my nuclear plant did that. And now that you know, your life is in danger.
jokes.setup: Lisa's innocent comment about green river
jokes.punchline: Homer casually admits to environmental crime then threatens her
jokes.explanation: Perfectly structured escalation from admission to threat, very Homer-like casual evil

jokes.text: I, Joseph Fitzgerald O'Malley Fitzpatrick O'Donnell the Edge Quimby
jokes.setup: Mayor introducing himself at Irish celebration
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously long fake Irish name with 'the Edge' thrown in
jokes.explanation: Classic Quimby pandering with absurd name, 'the Edge' reference adds modern touch

jokes.text: The Irish gave the world the paddy wagon, the shamrock shake and folk dancing where you don't move your arms.
jokes.setup: Listing Irish contributions to world culture
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and ridiculous 'contributions' including corporate products
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from serious to corporate to absurdly specific physical detail

jokes.text: Where's the IRA when you need 'em? We renounced the ways of the gun and the bomb.
jokes.setup: Willie complaining about no-alcohol policy
jokes.punchline: Casually wishing for terrorist intervention, then admitting they're reformed
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with good reversal - calls for violence then admits they're peaceful now

jokes.text: Two kinds of Irish people? What are they fighting over, who gets to sleep in the bathtub?
jokes.setup: Homer noticing Northern Irish parade
jokes.punchline: Crude assumption about Irish poverty stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer ignorance but relies on cheap stereotypes about Irish poverty

jokes.text: It always comes down to transubstantiation versus consubstantiation.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Irish Catholic/Protestant conflict
jokes.punchline: Moe using sophisticated theological terms incorrectly
jokes.explanation: Brilliant contrast - Moe using complex theological concepts while completely missing the point of sectarian conflict

jokes.text: Without the booze, these guys all remember how much they hate each other!
jokes.setup: Alcohol-free St. Patrick's Day turns violent
jokes.punchline: Alcohol was actually keeping the peace, not causing problems
jokes.explanation: Good ironic reversal - the supposed solution created the problem it was meant to solve

jokes.text: This was such a pleasant St. Patrick's Day till the Irish people showed up.
jokes.setup: Violence breaks out at Irish celebration
jokes.punchline: Blaming Irish people for ruining their own holiday
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurd logic - blaming the people the holiday is meant to celebrate

jokes.text: You don't have the teeth for it. Aye, but we could gum the frosting.
jokes.setup: Old man tells kids they can't eat stolen cupcakes
jokes.punchline: Kids admit they're toothless but offer alternative eating method
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay with 'gum' as verb, plays on toothless Irish stereotype

jokes.text: Light, moist, and such a marvelous shape retention.
jokes.setup: Baker tasting Marge's cupcake
jokes.punchline: Overly technical praise focusing on 'shape retention' - hints at erotic bakery
jokes.explanation: Subtle foreshadowing - 'shape retention' becomes meaningful when we learn about erotic bakery

jokes.text: A professional baker's employee? Imagine how different my life would be.
jokes.setup: Marge offered a job at bakery
jokes.punchline: Treats basic employment as life-changing career advancement
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Marge's low expectations and humble nature

jokes.text: I'll use my one phone call to find out all about it!
jokes.setup: Marge has great news, Homer is in jail
jokes.punchline: Homer will waste his one phone call on news instead of getting help
jokes.explanation: Pure Homer logic - prioritizing curiosity over practical need for bail

jokes.text: Mulk request change of venue. Green monster cannot get fair trial in downtown Springfield.
jokes.setup: Courtroom scene with various defendants
jokes.punchline: Hulk-like character speaking in broken English requesting venue change
jokes.explanation: Simple parody of Hulk with legal proceedings twist

jokes.text: Three-peat.
jokes.setup: Judge calls Homer a repeat offender
jokes.punchline: Homer uses basketball term 'three-peat' for three crimes
jokes.explanation: Nice wordplay showing Homer's casual attitude toward his criminal record

jokes.text: I make that in a year.
jokes.setup: Bail set at $25,000
jokes.punchline: Homer's low salary revealed through bail comparison
jokes.explanation: Efficient character joke revealing Homer's financial situation

jokes.text: I called your boss to verify your employment, and he says you're fired for getting arrested. You'd be amazed how often that happens.
jokes.setup: Standard employment verification process
jokes.punchline: Homer gets fired during the call, bondsman notes this is common
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how Homer's problems compound, plus commentary on bail system

jokes.text: I also take a tooth, make a plaster cast, put it on this necklace, then replace the tooth at my expense.
jokes.setup: Bounty hunter explaining his methods
jokes.punchline: Elaborate tooth-collecting ritual that he pays for himself
jokes.explanation: Absurd professional quirk with unexpected courtesy of paying for replacement

jokes.text: You have to pass an online exam. No, wait, they got rid of that. There is a $10 filing fee, but you can get around it. Congratulations! You're now a bounty hunter!
jokes.setup: Homer asking about bounty hunter requirements
jokes.punchline: Requirements get progressively easier until there are none at all
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing deregulation absurdity - each requirement disappears until certification is meaningless

jokes.text: Bail-jumpers welcome! That's refreshing, after the cold shoulder we got at the Townhomes at Sterling Pointe.
jokes.setup: Fugitives see Homer's trap advertising
jokes.punchline: Previous upscale housing discriminated against them
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between criminal status and middle-class housing expectations

jokes.text: Now, let's think about this. If you shoot me, I won't be able to stop you, and you'll be free to go. But someone may come after you. Probably not, given your reputation for shooting people who come after you.
jokes.setup: Homer confronting armed fugitive
jokes.punchline: Homer talks himself out of his own argument through logical progression
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic - starts with reasonable argument then undermines himself through overthinking

jokes.text: What I'm trying to say is not shooting me now would be the biggest mistake of your life.
jokes.setup: Homer's backwards logic about why he shouldn't be shot
jokes.punchline: Concludes by telling criminal that NOT shooting him would be a mistake
jokes.explanation: Peak Homer - completely backwards logic delivered with total confidence, essentially arguing for his own death

jokes.text: Bullet-proof glass. Flanders?!
jokes.setup: Homer survives gunshot unexpectedly
jokes.punchline: Ned Flanders coincidentally installing bullet-proof glass saves Homer
jokes.explanation: Solid coincidence rescue with good surprise reveal of Ned's involvement

jokes.text: Young man, what would your mother say if she knew you were shooting nice people in the brain?
jokes.setup: Ned addressing armed criminal
jokes.punchline: Polite, parental scolding for attempted murder
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned Flanders - treating deadly violence like a parenting issue with his characteristic politeness

jokes.text: She'd say that year off from Princeton was the worst decision I ever made.
jokes.setup: Criminal asked what his mother would think
jokes.punchline: Reveals privileged background, mother's disappointment about education not crime
jokes.explanation: Great subversion - mother cares more about education than criminal behavior, reveals class background

jokes.text: Homer, you could have killed him! I sure could have, thanks to you.
jokes.setup: Ned criticizing Homer's dangerous tactics
jokes.punchline: Homer takes it as a compliment and credits Ned
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer misunderstanding - takes criticism as praise and credits his critic

jokes.text: Now I can clearly see potential customers walk right past my store. Enjoy your shopping elsewhere!
jokes.setup: Ned's new bullet-proof glass window
jokes.punchline: Better visibility only allows him to watch customers avoid his store
jokes.explanation: Good ironic twist - technological improvement makes business problems more visible

jokes.text: As the salad said to the soup, 'I'm all mixed up.'
jokes.setup: Ned expressing confusion about partnership with Homer
jokes.punchline: Painfully wholesome food-based metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfectly dorky Ned humor - the wholesomeness is part of the joke

jokes.text: And you have to promise no diddilies or doodilies. My friend, you have a deal-arooney! D'oh!
jokes.setup: Ned makes Homer promise not to use his characteristic speech patterns
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately uses similar cutesy suffix and realizes his mistake
jokes.explanation: Perfect character comedy - Homer can't help adopting Ned's speech pattern while trying to avoid it

jokes.text: Why do you only have me make spheres, rods, and half-moon shapes?
jokes.setup: Marge questions the specific shapes she's asked to bake
jokes.punchline: Innocently describes phallic and anatomical shapes without realizing
jokes.explanation: Good setup for erotic bakery reveal - Marge's innocence contrasts with obvious implications

jokes.text: We need something tasty and tasteless. I've got just the thing, courtesy of my newest baker: freshly-frosted ass cake.
jokes.setup: Customer requesting something for bachelorette party
jokes.punchline: Baker offers literal 'ass cake' - wordplay on 'tasteless'
jokes.explanation: Standard double entendre - 'tasteless' meaning both flavorless and crude

jokes.text: This is an erotic bakery!
jokes.setup: Marge's gradual realization about the bakery's true nature
jokes.punchline: Her shocked discovery and reaction
jokes.explanation: Payoff to building revelation - Marge's innocent shock works for her character

jokes.text: For as long as there's been baking, there's been erotic baking. It fills a need and harms no one.
jokes.setup: Marge's moral objections to erotic bakery
jokes.punchline: Baker treats it as natural, ancient tradition
jokes.explanation: Reasonable argument delivered seriously about absurd topic

jokes.text: Without your baking, people like me will be forced into back alleys, repurposing Ho Hos and Ding Dongs.
jokes.setup: Baker arguing for importance of professional erotic baking
jokes.punchline: Describes amateur alternatives using innuendo-named snack cakes
jokes.explanation: Clever use of actual product names that sound sexual, escalates to ridiculous extreme

jokes.text: Finally, a practical use for electricity.
jokes.setup: Homer playing with Taser
jokes.punchline: Suggests electricity hasn't been useful until now
jokes.explanation: Pure Homer logic - ignoring all other uses of electricity to focus on weapon application

jokes.text: No. It looks cool! D'oh!
jokes.setup: Ned asks Homer to remove Taser from pants
jokes.punchline: Homer refuses then immediately tasers himself
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy - Homer's vanity leads to self-inflicted pain

jokes.text: Shell casings! There's still bullets in some of these. Point them away from your sisters when you hammer them.
jokes.setup: Father giving son 'presents' from his criminal work
jokes.punchline: Casual safety advice about live ammunition as toy
jokes.explanation: Dark family comedy - treating dangerous evidence as children's gifts with minimal safety precautions

jokes.text: Dad, is this from a meth lab? The biggest one in town. This is evidence. Evidence that I love my little girl.
jokes.setup: Daughter questions chemistry set's origin
jokes.punchline: Father admits criminal source then reframes as love gesture
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay on 'evidence' - literal criminal evidence becomes evidence of affection

jokes.text: Yes, for now.
jokes.setup: Marge gives Homer picture frame with kids' photos
jokes.punchline: Homer's ominous response about temporary nature
jokes.explanation: Dark implication about children's future delivered with Homer's casual menace

jokes.text: I like their Christian cover band: AD/BC. Kindly deeds done for free.
jokes.setup: Homer mentions liking AC/DC
jokes.punchline: Ned's Christian alternative band with wholesome lyrics
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Ned joke - perfect Christian alternative to AC/DC with clever name flip and wholesome lyric parody

jokes.text: Man, that's harder to swallow than evolution.
jokes.setup: Homer showing unusual way to eat pizza
jokes.punchline: Criminal compares difficulty to accepting evolution
jokes.explanation: Quick character note - criminal is religious fundamentalist, uses evolution as comparison

jokes.text: Lord, in your mercy, could you give my friend a stroke?
jokes.setup: Ned praying during dangerous chase
jokes.punchline: Politely asks God to harm Homer
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark Ned moment - maintains polite prayer format while requesting violence against partner

jokes.text: Quick, we'll have to eat from each end. We'll know we're safe when our lips meet in the middle.
jokes.setup: Skinner and Chalmers hiding behind erotic cake
jokes.punchline: Practical solution creates romantic implication
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy setup with accidental romantic subtext between the characters

jokes.text: You need a special sticker just to bring a bike.
jokes.setup: Homer driving car into subway
jokes.punchline: Ned notes bureaucratic requirements for much smaller violations
jokes.explanation: Good observation about bureaucratic priorities - major violations easier than minor ones

jokes.text: Wait, I left a sandwich in there.
jokes.setup: Homer about to put criminal in body bag
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of food storage in evidence bag
jokes.explanation: Pure Homer - treating professional equipment as lunch storage

jokes.text: I'll tell you what's over. This partnership!
jokes.setup: Ned saying partnership is over
jokes.punchline: Homer tries to one-up him with same statement
jokes.explanation: Mild Homer moment - trying to have last word with identical statement

jokes.text: You know, if you take your foot off the accelerator going downhill, you wouldn't use so much gas.
jokes.setup: Tense moment after partnership ends
jokes.punchline: Ned offers helpful driving tip despite conflict
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned - can't help being helpful even when angry, shows his fundamental nature

jokes.text: I hid it all in the one place you'd never look.
jokes.setup: Marge confessing to hiding erotic baked goods
jokes.punchline: Implies Homer never looks in certain locations
jokes.explanation: Standard marital joke about husband not noticing things around house

jokes.text: I can see you withholding sex or withholding cake, but withholding sexy cake?
jokes.setup: Homer learning about Marge's erotic baking deception
jokes.punchline: Combines his two primary interests into ultimate betrayal
jokes.explanation: Great escalation combining Homer's two obsessions - treats combination as worst possible scenario

jokes.text: You, me and the cake. First, why don't you and I and the cake take a shower?
jokes.setup: Homer suggesting they go to bed with cake
jokes.punchline: Escalates to bizarre threesome scenario with baked good
jokes.explanation: Homer's sexual obsession with food taken to logical extreme - treating cake as third party

jokes.text: And how did you know I wouldn't be there? Lucky guess.
jokes.setup: Homer asks how Ned knew he'd skip Lisa's recital
jokes.punchline: Ned's sarcastic response about Homer's predictable neglect
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Ned's dry sarcasm about Homer's parenting failures

jokes.text: Forgot my keys.
jokes.setup: Homer dramatically declares Ned will never take him alive
jokes.punchline: Immediately has to return for forgotten keys
jokes.explanation: Classic deflation - dramatic moment undercut by mundane necessity

jokes.text: You hang onto resentment like a Confederate widow.
jokes.setup: Homer and Ned reconciling
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and historically inappropriate comparison
jokes.explanation: Brilliant specific comparison - historically informed but inappropriately applied

jokes.text: Yeah, but you hold on to the big things.
jokes.setup: Ned mentions forgiving Homer for killing his wife
jokes.punchline: Homer dismisses wife's death as not worth holding grudge over
jokes.explanation: Dark callback to major series event - Homer's casual dismissal of causing death shows his sociopathy

jokes.text: Thanks, but... what are you holding on to?
jokes.setup: Ned saves Homer from falling
jokes.punchline: Both realize Ned has no anchor and they're both falling
jokes.explanation: Standard action comedy - heroic rescue undermined by logical flaw

jokes.text: I guess I did know the whole Old Testament by heart. Out with the old, in with the new. Testament, that is.
jokes.setup: Ned finishing reciting Old Testament while trapped
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'out with old, in with new' referring to biblical testaments
jokes.explanation: Good Ned pun showing his biblical knowledge and wordplay skills even in crisis

jokes.text: Make it life, and you've got a deal.
jokes.setup: Prosecutor threatening long prison sentence
jokes.punchline: Homer negotiates for longer sentence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer absurdity - treats worse punishment as better deal, suggests home life issues

tmdb_overview: Bounty hunter Ned is forced to scrap his partnership with Homer when the hapless layabout skips bail, and Marge mistakenly takes a job at an erotic bakery.
